industry.headline: Delhi simplifies net metering rules for rooftop solar
industry.summary: The Delhi Electricity Regulatory Commission (DERC) amended net metering guidelines for rooftop solar projects by replacing the existing three-tier rooftop framework. The change simplifies the rules for residential and other rooftop solar installations under DERC's updated regulations.

industry.headline: Coal India names winner for 750 MWh storage consultancy
industry.summary: Coal India announced the winner of an auction to appoint an owner's engineer-cum-engineering consultancy firm for an 187.5 MW/750 MWh battery energy storage system at Telangana Transmission Corporation's Choutuppal substation. The consultancy supports the specified BESS project.

industry.headline: Rajasthan Tenders 172 MW/688 MWh Battery Energy Storage Projects
industry.summary: The three distribution companies (DISCOMs) of Rajasthan have invited bids to procure power from pilot-based, small-scale, standalone decentralized battery energy storage systems totaling 172 MW/688 MWh under a ‘Build-Own-Operate’ model.

industry.headline: MNRE extends ALMM cell exemption for net metering, open access solar
industry.summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) extended the exemption from ALMM List II requirement for solar cells for net metering and open access renewable energy projects commissioned on or before December 31, 2026. The exemption applies to specified project categories under the revised timeline.

tech.headline: ASEAN shifts focus to battery manufacturing and EV adoption
tech.summary: ASEAN shifts focus to battery manufacturing and scaling EV adoption while integrating grids, highlighted by the upcoming 4th ASEAN Battery Technology Conference in Sepang, Malaysia next month. The rapid transition of Southeast Asia toward clean energy moves from early electric mobility adoption to regional industrialization, with the conference emphasizing engineering solutions and policy framework proposals toward engineering focus.

tech.headline: Siemens Energy to merge brands into Omterra
tech.summary: Siemens Energy will merge Siemens Energy and Siemens Gamesa Renewable Energy brands into Omterra, rebranding next year after its 2020 spin‑off, while retaining the licensed Siemens Energy name. The firm aims to streamline operations, boost brand recognition, and leverage synergies in the global renewable energy market. This move supports its goal of accelerating clean energy deployment worldwide.
tech.searchQuery: Siemens to unite energy businesses under new brand 2026

tech.headline: Sol Systems Launches 123-MW Solar Project in Illinois
tech.summary: Sol Systems began construction on a 123-MWAC solar portfolio in Illinois after securing financing. The projects span Knox, Tazewell, and Peoria Counties across 900+ acres. This development reflects the company's capacity to execute large-scale solar initiatives with stable capital structures. Specific timelines or community impacts are not detailed, but the focus is on expanding renewable energy capacity in the region.

learn.title: Flow Batteries for Grid Storage
learn.intro: Flow batteries store energy in liquid electrolytes, enabling long-duration grid storage. Vanadium and iron systems decouple power and capacity, supporting renewable integration. They reduce curtailment of solar and wind, stabilize grids, and provide resilience. As clean energy grows, such storage is vital for balancing variable supply and demand affordably.
learn.c1_text: Flow batteries use two liquid tanks of electrolyte pumped through a cell. Like a fuel tank separate from engine, energy capacity scales with tank size. Vanadium systems last 20+ years with 100% depth of discharge, unlike lithium-ion which degrades faster under deep cycling.
learn.c2_text: Vanadium redox flow batteries avoid cross-contamination and tolerate daily cycling. Iron-flow uses abundant low-cost iron, cutting material expense. Both separate power (stack size) from energy (tank volume), enabling 4–12 hour discharge for grid firming at utility scale.
learn.c3_text: India's draft national storage mission targets 47 GWh batteries by 2030. Flow systems suit state DISCOMs needing long-duration backup. Pilot vanadium installations in Rajasthan explore desert solar pairing, reducing diesel use during evening peaks across weak rural feeders.
learn.stat2: Iron-flow cuts electrolyte cost by ~60% vs vanadium

learn.title: Bifacial Solar Panels
learn.intro: Bifacial solar panels capture sunlight on both front and rear surfaces, boosting yield from reflected ground light. They raise plant output without extra land, cutting costs. As global solar expands, bifacial tech improves competitiveness and energy density of utility-scale farms worldwide.
learn.c1_text: Bifacial panels have transparent backs, letting albedo light hit rear cells. Like a mirror reflecting sunshine onto your back, white gravel or sand bounces photons. Rear gain adds 5–20% energy versus monofacial, depending on surface reflectivity and mounting height.
learn.c2_text: Higher mounting and trackers increase rear exposure. Anti-reflective glass and cell spacing optimize capture. Technical specs show bifaciality factor of 70–90%, meaning rear makes that fraction of front output under ideal diffuse and reflected conditions.
learn.c3_text: India's Bhadla solar park uses bifacial modules on sandy terrain, gaining ~12% output. With 90 GW solar installed (2024), MNRE promotes bifacial in competitive bids to lower levelized cost for DISCOMs facing peak deficits in summer.

learn.title: Green Bonds for Climate Finance
learn.intro: Green bonds let renewable projects raise capital from global investors with labelled climate benefits. They lower financing costs and scale wind, solar and storage. As transitions need trillions, such instruments channel pension and sovereign funds into clean energy reliably.
learn.c1_text: Green bonds are debt securities funding eco projects. Like a tagged savings jar, proceeds must build solar farms not coal. Issuers pay fixed returns; investors get impact reports. In 2023, global issuance hit $590 billion across certified frameworks.
learn.c2_text: Multilateral banks co-guarantee to de-risk emerging market issues. Technical standards (ICMA) verify additionality. This unlocks pools like EU pensions for 15-year RE loans, cutting weighted cost of capital by 20–50 basis points versus unlabelled debt.
learn.c3_text: India's sovereign green bond raised ₹160 billion in 2023 for solar and rail. SECI uses proceeds for 1.5 GW parks. RBI guidelines align with IEA findings, attracting foreign institutional investors to Indian renewable pipelines.
learn.stat4: SECI funds 1.5 GW via green bonds

learn.title: Renewable Purchase Obligation India
learn.intro: Renewable Purchase Obligation mandates DISCOMs buy a fixed share of power from clean sources. It drives demand certainty for developers and meets climate targets. For Indian utilities, RPO compliance is key to affordable decarbonization and avoiding penalties from regulators.
learn.c1_text: RPO requires electricity distributors to source minimum renewable percentage. Like a quota for greens in your diet, DISCOMs must buy certificates or direct power. Non-compliance draws penalties from state commissions, ensuring market pull for solar and wind.
learn.c2_text: DISCOMs surrender Renewable Energy Certificates equal to obligation. Technical tracking via national registry avoids double counting. RPO trajectories rise yearly; 2024 target ~29% including hydro, with solar-specific carve-outs to balance generation mixes.
learn.c3_text: MNRE's 2024 RPO roadmap sets 43% by 2030. Tamil Nadu DISCOMs procured 10 GW renewables via RPO. Weak compliance earlier caused ₹12k crore certificate shortfall; new penalties improved monthly reporting across states.

learn.title: Solar Panel Types Compared
learn.intro: Monocrystalline, polycrystalline and thin-film panels differ in efficiency, cost and use. Knowing trade-offs helps homeowners and utilities choose right tech. As solar dominates new capacity, selecting panels affects land, yield and payback across diverse climates and budgets.
learn.c1_text: Monocrystalline cells are single silicon crystals, ~22% efficient, black and pricier. Poly uses melted fragments, ~17% efficient, blue. Like whole vs crushed ice, mono performs better in heat and space-limited roofs, poly suits large cheap land arrays.
learn.c2_text: Thin-film deposits amorphous silicon or CdTe on glass, ~12–16% efficient but flexible and light. Technical merit: better shade tolerance and low light. NREL tests show lower degradation, ideal for building-integrated or portable Indian applications.
learn.c3_text: India's rooftop segment favors mono for 20% space save. NREL data informs BIS standards. Kolkata warehouses use thin-film on weak roofs; utility parks in Gujarat mostly poly till 2022 price drop shifted to mono PERC.

learn.title: Second Life EV Batteries
learn.intro: Retired EV batteries retain 70–80% capacity, ideal for grid storage. Reusing delays recycling and cuts costs for renewables integration. As electric vehicles scale in India and globally, second-life packs can stabilize grids and boost circular economy value.
learn.c1_text: EV batteries fade to 80% after 1500 cycles, unfit for cars but fine for static use. Like hand-me-down phones, they serve solar buffering. IRENA notes repurposing saves 30–60% versus new storage, easing utility capital strain.
learn.c2_text: Modules are regrouped, balanced and paired with inverters. Second-life systems provide 2–4 hour backup at 0.3–0.5 $/kWh lifecycle cost. Thermal management differs from EV; stationary racks simplify cooling versus moving vehicles.
learn.c3_text: Tata Power reuses Nexon packs for Mumbai peak shaving. With 4 million EVs expected by 2030, MNRE study models 30 GWh second-life stock. Ashok Leyland buses feed Bengaluru microgrids, cutting diesel at IT parks.
